title: United Kingdom Marriage Allowance Transfer
description: Transfer part of an individual's Personal Allowance to their spouse or civil partner under Marriage Allowance, using the official HMRC service. The lower earner applies. This document describes both partners' identity and National Insurance numbers, the relationship, the identity-verification evidence, and an optional backdating start, that the service collects. It does not submit the request; the live HMRC source is always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md). Income thresholds and the transferable amount are intentionally not encoded.

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from the official GOV.UK 'Marriage Allowance' guidance page. Confirmed against the source: eligibility (the applicant is the lower earner who pays no Income Tax or earns below the Personal Allowance, and the partner is a basic-rate taxpayer); the applicant applies; married or civil-partnership status; and the ability to backdate up to 4 tax years. The Personal Allowance figure (£12,570) and the basic-rate band are described, not encoded, because they are set each tax year (cf. the time-versioning treatment in gb/dvla/vehicle-tax). HMRC requires identity verification using one of several evidence types (e.g. UK passport, recent payslip, P60); this is modelled as an enum. Individual input fields reflect the known HMRC flow but the screen-by-screen field order and labels have not yet been independently captured. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: applicantFirstName
fields.label: Your first name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The lower earner (the partner transferring allowance) is the applicant.

fields.name: applicantLastName
fields.label: Your last name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: applicantDateOfBirth
fields.label: Your date of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. YYYY-MM-DD.

fields.name: applicantNationalInsuranceNumber
fields.label: Your National Insurance number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Two letters, six digits, and a final letter (A-D), without spaces, e.g. QQ123456C.
fields.sourceRef: Marriage Allowance — Your National Insurance number

fields.name: applicantIdentityEvidence
fields.label: How will you confirm your identity?
fields.description: HMRC asks for one piece of evidence to confirm your identity. uk_passport = a valid UK passport; recent_payslip = one of your last 3 months' payslips; p60 = your most recent P60; self_assessment = details from a recent Self Assessment tax return; tax_credits = your tax credit claim details; northern_ireland_driving_licence = a Northern Ireland driving licence. Provide the matching evidence to the live service; GovSchema does not transport the document.

fields.name: relationshipType
fields.label: Are you married or in a civil partnership?
fields.description: You must be married or in a civil partnership to claim; living together is not sufficient.

fields.name: applicantIsLowerEarner
fields.label: I am the lower earner (I pay no Income Tax or earn below my Personal Allowance)
fields.description: Marriage Allowance is claimed by the lower earner, who transfers part of their Personal Allowance to their partner. The Personal Allowance amount is set each tax year and is shown on the live source; it is not encoded here.

fields.name: partnerFirstName
fields.label: Partner's first name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The higher earner receiving the transferred allowance.

fields.name: partnerLastName
fields.label: Partner's last name
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: partnerDateOfBirth
fields.label: Partner's date of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. YYYY-MM-DD.

fields.name: partnerNationalInsuranceNumber
fields.label: Partner's National Insurance number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Two letters, six digits, and a final letter (A-D), without spaces, e.g. QQ123456C. Your partner must be a basic-rate Income Tax payer.
fields.sourceRef: Marriage Allowance — Partner's National Insurance number

fields.name: backdateClaim
fields.label: Do you want to backdate your claim?
fields.description: You can backdate a claim to include any tax year since 5 April 2022 (up to 4 previous tax years) in which you were eligible. If true, the service collects the earliest tax year to include.

fields.name: backdateFromTaxYearStart
fields.label: Earliest tax year to backdate to (start year)
fields.description: Required only when backdateClaim is true: the calendar year in which the earliest tax year to include began (a UK tax year runs 6 April of this year to 5 April of the next). For example, enter 2022 for the 2022 to 2023 tax year.
